question: Find the characteristic polynomial and eigenvalues of the 2×2 matrix A = [[3, 1], [1, 3]]
expectedPoints: Eigenvalues are λ = 2 and λ = 4 from solving characteristic equation

question: State and prove the Cayley-Hamilton Theorem for a 2×2 matrix
expectedPoints: State: Every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic polynomial
For 2×2, characteristic polynomial p(λ) = λ² - tr(A)λ + det(A)
Show p(A) = A² - tr(A)A + det(A)I = 0 by direct computation using A = [[a,b],[c,d]]

question: Using Sylvester's determinant theorem, compute det([2 1 1; 1 2 1; 1 1 2])
Find the minimal polynomial of A = [[1,1,0],[0,1,0],[0,0,2]]
If A is a 3×3 matrix with eigenvalues 1, 2, 3, find det(A), tr(A), and characteristic polynomial
Prove that similar matrices have the same characteristic polynomial
expectedPoints: Let B = P⁻¹AP for invertible P
Compute characteristic polynomial of B: det(B - λI) = det(P⁻¹AP - λI)
= det(P⁻¹) × det(A - λI) × det(P) = det(A - λI) since det(P⁻¹)det(P) = 1

question: Find all eigenvalues of block diagonal matrix diag([[1,2],[2,4]], [[0,1],[0,0]])
expectedPoints: Second block [[0,1],[0,0]]: characteristic polynomial λ², eigenvalue λ = 0 with multiplicity 2

question: For the matrix A = [[2,1,1],[1,2,1],[1,1,2]], find: (i) Characteristic polynomial (ii) Eigenvalues (iii) Minimal polynomial (iv) Verify Cayley-Hamilton theorem
expectedPoints: (iv) Substitute A in characteristic polynomial: p(A) = A³ - 6A² + 9A - 4I = 0

question: Using block matrix methods, derive the formula for the inverse of a 2×2 block matrix [A B; C D] where all blocks are square and A is invertible. Apply to find inverse of [[2 1 1; 0 2 1; 0 0 2], [1 0 0; 0 1 0; 0 0 1]; [0 0 0; 1 0 0; 0 1 0]]
expectedPoints: When A is invertible: [A B; C D]⁻¹ = [A⁻¹ + A⁻¹B(D-CA⁻¹B)⁻¹CA⁻¹, -A⁻¹B(D-CA⁻¹B)⁻¹; -(D-CA⁻¹B)⁻¹CA⁻¹, (D-CA⁻¹B)⁻¹]
This uses Schur complement: inverse = [A⁻¹ + A⁻¹BSA⁻¹, -A⁻¹BS⁻¹; -S⁻¹CA⁻¹, S⁻¹] where S = D - CA⁻¹B
Compute A⁻¹ using formula for upper triangular
Compute Schur complement S = D - CA⁻¹B

question: State and prove the relationship between characteristic polynomial and minimal polynomial. Find minimal polynomial of A = [[4,1,2],[0,4,1],[0,0,4]] and verify that it divides characteristic polynomial
expectedPoints: Relationship: (i) Minimal polynomial divides characteristic polynomial (ii) Both have same distinct roots (eigenvalues) (iii) Minimal polynomial degree ≤ characteristic polynomial degree
For given A: Characteristic polynomial = det([[4-λ,1,2],[0,4-λ,1],[0,0,4-λ]]) = (4-λ)³ = -(λ-4)³
This equals characteristic polynomial (they're equal in this case)

question: Let A and B be n×n matrices. Prove that A and B are similar if and only if there exist invertible matrix P such that B = P⁻¹AP. Using this, show that similar matrices have same eigenvalues, determinant, and trace
expectedPoints: (=>) If A and B similar, exists P with B = P⁻¹AP
(<=) If B = P⁻¹AP for invertible P, then A and B similar by definition
Same eigenvalues: char poly of B = det(P⁻¹AP - λI) = det(P⁻¹(A-λI)P) = det(A-λI)
Same trace: tr(B) = tr(P⁻¹AP) = tr(APP⁻¹) = tr(A)
